point-01.title: Placement changes the price of the same tile
point-01.lesson: Placement changes the value of the discard. A leading or fragile seat can make future defense more valuable than a prettier immediate shape.
point-01.prompt: Before choosing, name the hand's job: win now, protect placement, escape last, or keep future exits.
point-01.answer: Copy the risk budget and table job. The correct discard serves the placement goal while leaving a playable next turn.

guide.caption: LuckyJ 6m, Nishiki 9s
guide.situation: South 2-0, middle hand, 37 tiles left. LuckyJ is lead on 35300 points in rank 1. After drawing [[9s]], LuckyJ cuts [[6m]]; Nishiki's top line is [[9s]]. Result: toimen won, self delta -4000
guide.read: The lead seat changes the price of this discard. LuckyJ chooses the discard that leaves the next turn manageable. The kept [[9s]] is river-safe against the right player (no called threat). Hibakari also chooses [[6m]], so read this as LuckyJ/Hibakari versus Nishiki, not LuckyJ against every NAGA head.
guide.whyNot: The Nishiki line through [[9s]] is tempting because it leaves 1-shanten, 16 visible ukeire, main accepts [[5m]], [[8m]], [[4p]], [[6p]], [[7p]]. LuckyJ's [[6m]] line leaves 2-shanten, 36 visible ukeire, main accepts [[5m]], [[6m]], [[7m]], [[8m]], [[9m]]. LuckyJ buys immediate safety: LuckyJ 0.0%, Nishiki 36.3%.
guide.copy: Copy the reason: if cutting [[6m]] keeps the hand's real job clearer than the Nishiki line through [[9s]], the choice is reproducible.
guide.limit: Copy the surface tile only when the score job, threat level, and next discard chain match.
guide.prompt: With 37 tiles left, would you cut [[6m]] or [[9s]]? First name what [[9s]] is preserving.
guide.answer: LuckyJ chooses [[6m]]. The reproducible reason is: The lead seat changes the price of this discard. LuckyJ chooses the discard that leaves the next turn manageable.

guide_ja.caption: LuckyJ 6m、Nishiki 9s
guide_ja.situation: South 2-0、middle、残り37枚。lead、35300点、着順1。LuckyJ は [[9s]] ツモから [[6m]] を切り、Nishiki 第一候補は [[9s]]。結果: toimen won, self delta -4000
guide_ja.read: lead では同じ牌の値段が変わる。LuckyJ は次巡も困りにくい打牌を選んでいる。 残る [[9s]] は 下家 に対して現物で、相手の状態は静かな相手。 Hibakari も [[6m]] を第一候補にしており、この例は LuckyJ/Hibakari 対 Nishiki の分岐として読む。
guide_ja.whyNot: Nishiki の [[9s]] は 1シャンテン、見えている受け入れ16枚、主な受け [[5m]], [[8m]], [[4p]], [[6p]], [[7p]] を残すので魅力がある。LuckyJ の [[6m]] は 2シャンテン、見えている受け入れ36枚、主な受け [[5m]], [[6m]], [[7m]], [[8m]], [[9m]]。LuckyJ は即時危険を下げている。LuckyJ 0.0%、Nishiki 36.3%。
guide_ja.copy: [[6m]] 切りが [[9s]] 経由より手の仕事をはっきり残すなら、その理由を真似できる。
guide_ja.limit: 表面の牌を真似する条件は、点数状況、脅威、次に切る牌がそろう時である。
guide_ja.prompt: 残り37枚で [[6m]] と [[9s]] のどちらを切るか。先に [[9s]] が何を残しているかを言う。
guide_ja.answer: LuckyJ は [[6m]] を選ぶ。理由は lead では同じ牌の値段が変わる。LuckyJ は次巡も困りにくい打牌を選んでいる。

point-03.title: Bad closed shapes buy calls
point-03.lesson: The call is valuable when the closed route is mostly imaginary and the open hand creates yaku, tempo, or real tenpai pressure.
point-03.prompt: Before calling, say what the call creates: yaku, tenpai, real 1-shanten, denial, or safe draw equity.
point-03.answer: Call poor closed shapes when the exposed hand has a clear purpose and still leaves a way to stop.

guide.caption: pon on 7m, then 4m
guide.situation: South 3-1, late hand, 1 tiles left. LuckyJ is above starting score on 25200 points in rank 2. LuckyJ calls pon on [[7m]] from the left player, then cuts [[4m]]. Result: draw, self delta +1500
guide.read: The closed route is running out of practical turns; after the pon and [[4m]], the hand reaches tenpai.
guide.whyNot: Passing is tempting because it keeps the hand closed. With 1 tiles left, the closed ideal may run out of useful turns.
guide.copy: Copy the pon only when it creates yaku, speed, tenpai pressure, or denial, and when the first post-call discard [[4m]] is already planned.
guide.limit: The call needs a named post-call discard and a next defensive exit to become LuckyJ-style tempo.
guide.prompt: Would you pon on [[7m]]? Before answering, name the discard after the call.
guide.answer: The first check is the post-call discard: [[4m]] is already the release tile. The closed route is running out of practical turns; after the pon and [[4m]], the hand reaches tenpai.

guide_ja.caption: pon して 4m
guide_ja.situation: South 3-1、late、残り1枚。above starting score、25200点、着順2。LuckyJ は 上家 から [[7m]] を pon し、[[4m]] を切る。結果: draw, self delta +1500
guide_ja.read: 閉じた手が間に合いにくい局面で、pon 後の [[4m]] まで決めると テンパイ になる。
guide_ja.whyNot: 門前維持は自然な選択。ただしこの局面では残り枚数と手牌 7m 7m から、門前の理想形を待つ余裕が薄い。
guide_ja.copy: pon が役、速度、テンパイ、または相手への圧力を作る時だけ真似する。鳴いた後の最初の打牌 [[4m]] まで先に決める。
guide_ja.limit: 鳴いた後の安全牌や次の方針まで言える時に、LuckyJ 型のテンポになる。
guide_ja.prompt: [[7m]] を pon するか。答える前に、鳴いた後に何を切るかを言う。
guide_ja.answer: 最初に見るのは鳴き後の打牌で、この例では [[4m]] まで見えている。閉じた手が間に合いにくい局面で、pon 後の [[4m]] まで決めると テンパイ になる。

point-04.title: Open hands must keep an exit

point-04.answer: A cheap exposed hand needs an exit so the next threat avoids a bad forced push.

point-05.title: Cut the future liability now
point-05.lesson: The floater is bad because a slow hand may be forced to discard it after the table becomes dangerous.
point-05.prompt: Find the tile you will hate discarding after the first riichi or second call.
point-05.answer: If the hand is far away and the tile has no value job, remove it before it becomes expensive.

guide.caption: LuckyJ 6p, Nishiki 1s
guide.situation: East 1-0, middle hand, 37 tiles left. LuckyJ is above starting score on 25000 points in rank 4. After drawing [[6p]], LuckyJ cuts [[6p]]; Nishiki's top line is [[1s]]. Result: shimocha won, self delta +0
guide.read: [[6p]] is the liability LuckyJ is willing to remove now. Waiting can force the same tile out after riichi or another call, when it costs more. The kept [[1s]] is river-safe and an outside tile (sotogawa) against the left player (no called threat). Hibakari also chooses [[6p]], so read this as LuckyJ/Hibakari versus Nishiki, not LuckyJ against every NAGA head.

point-07.title: Call for tempo with a purpose
point-07.lesson: The call has a job beyond completing blocks: speed up a poor hand, contest the table, break a bonus window, or preserve draw equity.
point-07.prompt: Name the purpose before exposing the hand.
point-07.answer: A good tempo call changes the round's clock and keeps you prepared for the next threat.

guide.caption: pon on W, then C
guide.situation: East 3-0, late hand, 24 tiles left. LuckyJ is above starting score on 27300 points in rank 2. LuckyJ calls pon on [[W]] from the left player, then cuts [[C]]. Result: toimen won, self delta -700
guide.read: The pon buys tempo with 24 tiles left. LuckyJ is changing the round clock and completing a useful set.
guide.whyNot: Passing is tempting because it keeps the hand closed. With 24 tiles left, the closed ideal may run out of useful turns.
guide.copy: Copy the pon only when it creates yaku, speed, tenpai pressure, or denial, and when the first post-call discard [[C]] is already planned.
guide.limit: The call needs a named post-call discard and a next defensive exit to become LuckyJ-style tempo.
guide.prompt: Would you pon on [[W]]? Before answering, name the discard after the call.
guide.answer: The first check is the post-call discard: [[C]] is already the release tile. The pon buys tempo with 24 tiles left. LuckyJ is changing the round clock and completing a useful set.

guide_ja.caption: pon して C
guide_ja.situation: East 3-0、late、残り24枚。above starting score、27300点、着順2。LuckyJ は 上家 から [[W]] を pon し、[[C]] を切る。結果: toimen won, self delta -700
guide_ja.read: pon は速度を買うための副露。残り24枚で、閉じたまま待つより局面の時計を進めている。
guide_ja.whyNot: 門前維持は自然な選択。ただしこの局面では残り枚数と手牌 W W から、門前の理想形を待つ余裕が薄い。
guide_ja.copy: pon が役、速度、テンパイ、または相手への圧力を作る時だけ真似する。鳴いた後の最初の打牌 [[C]] まで先に決める。
guide_ja.limit: 鳴いた後の安全牌や次の方針まで言える時に、LuckyJ 型のテンポになる。
guide_ja.prompt: [[W]] を pon するか。答える前に、鳴いた後に何を切るかを言う。
guide_ja.answer: 最初に見るのは鳴き後の打牌で、この例では [[C]] まで見えている。pon は速度を買うための副露。残り24枚で、閉じたまま待つより局面の時計を進めている。

point-08.title: Riichi converts pressure into equity
point-08.lesson: LuckyJ can declare before the shape is perfect when riichi itself changes the table. The declaration can force rivals to fold, deny calls, or lock in placement value.
point-08.prompt: Before riichi, ask who must now respond to you and whether better upgrades are realistic.
point-08.answer: If the wait is serviceable and the table must respect the stick, declaration pressure can be worth more than waiting.

players.hand: 2m 1p 9p 2s 9s 9s E S W P P F F

guide.caption: LuckyJ 1s, Nishiki 4s
guide.situation: East 4-0, early hand, 53 tiles left. LuckyJ is danger zone on 10100 points in rank 1. After drawing [[8s]], LuckyJ cuts [[1s]]; Nishiki's top line is [[4s]]. Result: self dealt into shimocha, delta -1300
guide.read: Riichi is part of the value calculation. With a declaration probability of 100.0%, LuckyJ treats pressure as a real output of the discard. The kept [[4s]] is suji against the across player (no called threat). Hibakari also chooses [[1s]], so read this as LuckyJ/Hibakari versus Nishiki, not LuckyJ against every NAGA head.
guide.whyNot: The Nishiki line through [[4s]] is tempting because it leaves 0-shanten, 3 visible ukeire, main accepts [[1s]], [[C]]. LuckyJ's [[1s]] line leaves 0-shanten, 4 visible ukeire, main accepts [[4s]], [[C]]. LuckyJ buys immediate safety: LuckyJ 0.0%, Nishiki 3.3%.
guide.copy: Copy the pressure only when riichi changes opponent behavior and the wait is good enough to make that pressure matter.
guide.limit: Declare with a reason. Bad waits, huge placement punishment, or a clear upgrade can still make damaten correct.
guide.prompt: With 53 tiles left, would you cut [[1s]] or [[4s]]? First name what [[4s]] is preserving.
guide.answer: LuckyJ chooses [[1s]]. The reproducible reason is: Riichi is part of the value calculation. With a declaration probability of 100.0%, LuckyJ treats pressure as a real output of the discard.

guide_ja.caption: LuckyJ 1s、Nishiki 4s
guide_ja.situation: East 4-0、early、残り53枚。danger zone、10100点、着順1。LuckyJ は [[8s]] ツモから [[1s]] を切り、Nishiki 第一候補は [[4s]]。結果: self dealt into shimocha, delta -1300
guide_ja.read: リーチ自体が価値になる局面。宣言確率 100.0% を含めて、圧力を打牌の成果として見ている。 残る [[4s]] は 対面 に対して筋で、相手の状態は静かな相手。 Hibakari も [[1s]] を第一候補にしており、この例は LuckyJ/Hibakari 対 Nishiki の分岐として読む。
guide_ja.whyNot: Nishiki の [[4s]] は 0シャンテン、見えている受け入れ3枚、主な受け [[1s]], [[C]] を残すので魅力がある。LuckyJ の [[1s]] は 0シャンテン、見えている受け入れ4枚、主な受け [[4s]], [[C]]。LuckyJ は即時危険を下げている。LuckyJ 0.0%、Nishiki 3.3%。
guide_ja.copy: リーチで相手の行動が変わり、待ちも最低限ある時だけ圧力を真似する。
guide_ja.limit: リーチには理由を持たせる。悪形、着順罰、明確な改良があればダマも残る。
guide_ja.prompt: 残り53枚で [[1s]] と [[4s]] のどちらを切るか。先に [[4s]] が何を残しているかを言う。
guide_ja.answer: LuckyJ は [[1s]] を選ぶ。理由は リーチ自体が価値になる局面。宣言確率 100.0% を含めて、圧力を打牌の成果として見ている。

point-08.title: Riichi converts pressure into equity
point-08.lesson: LuckyJ can declare before the shape is perfect when riichi itself changes the table. The declaration can force rivals to fold, deny calls, or lock in placement value.
point-08.prompt: Before riichi, ask who must now respond to you and whether better upgrades are realistic.
point-08.answer: If the wait is serviceable and the table must respect the stick, declaration pressure can be worth more than waiting.

point-09.title: Reprice the hand after every draw
point-09.lesson: A hand that was pushing one turn ago can become a fold when the next required discard is bad. The chain of future discards matters more than the current tile alone.
point-09.prompt: Count the current discard plus the next two likely discards before calling the hand a push.
point-09.answer: If the path contains several live danger tiles, downgrade the hand even when it is close.

point-13.title: Clean yakuhai against unproven open hands
point-13.lesson: When another player calls with an unclear yaku, singleton dragons and live value winds can become their missing yaku condition. LuckyJ often removes those tiles before they turn into the price of continuing.
point-13.prompt: After an opponent opens, ask which yakuhai still lets that hand win.
point-13.answer: When your hand is too slow to punish them and the yakuhai has no strong job, clean it before the open hand gets to use it.

point-15.title: Safe tiles expire
point-15.lesson: A tile that was safe earlier can become the correct discard once it no longer protects against the live danger or starts damaging the hand's real route.
point-15.prompt: Name the player this safe tile protects against, then ask whether that player is still the main danger.
point-15.answer: Spend stale safety when it is off-target, the hand is real, and another exit remains for the next bad draw.

point-16.title: Late outside cuts can preserve the route
point-16.lesson: Under pressure, cutting an edge or terminal can be the attacking discard because it keeps the middle tile that connects the actual path to tenpai.
point-16.prompt: When a terminal cut looks timid, check whether the inside tile is the hand's real connector.
point-16.answer: Treat the outside cut as route preservation when the outside tile carries little value or target-specific safety.

point-19.title: Leader safety is a qualifier
point-19.lesson: A lead lowers the ambition needed to end the hand, while each discard still needs to reduce real danger or preserve the next turn.

point-19.answer: Use the lead to lower the risk budget while still calculating shape, danger, and future exits.
